name: Razorpay SRE Dashboard — Prometheus & Grafana
scenario: Razorpay's on-call team has no visibility into payment service health. Build a Prometheus + Grafana observability stack: scrape configuration, 5 key PromQL queries, Grafana dashboard design, and alerting rules.

core.description: prometheus.yml: scrape payment-service from 2 instances every 15s. Add labels: app, team, environment.
Timer with publishPercentileHistogram=true. Counter with status+gateway tags. Gauge for retry queue depth.
5 PromQL queries: success rate, error%, correct multi-instance p99, queue depth, circuit breaker state.
prometheus/rules.yml: 2 alert rules — PaymentP99Spike (> 500ms for 5m) and PaymentErrorRate (> 1% for 5m). With for: duration and runbook_url.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): timer bucket count > 0, counter increments correctly, gauge reflects queue size. All 3 pass.
README: ASCII dashboard layout with 4 panel descriptions.

bonus.description: Recording rules: pre-compute error rate and p99. Verify they're faster than raw histogram_quantile.
SLI computation: availability%, latency SLI (% < 500ms), throughput SLI. Dashboard stat panels.
Error budget gauge: Grafana panel showing % of 30-day budget remaining. Alert: burn rate > 5×.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. publishPercentileHistogram() generates _bucket metrics required by histogram_quantile(). histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(metric_bucket[5m])) by (le)): the 'by (le)' is required — le is the less-than-or-equal bucket label. rate() requires a counter, not a gauge — queue depth (gauge) has no rate(), use the raw value. for: 5m in alert rules: prevents alerting on momentary spikes. Prometheus scrape_interval: 15s globally, override per job with scrape_interval: inside the job.
